summary: Convergence-bar scenario: operator supplies an inline CSV and asks for an LLM classification with structured output. The historical failure mode this catches is the model declaring a fixed CSV schema that omits one or more observed columns, which combined with on_validation_failure=discard silently drops every row. GREEN requires the source schema to cover the observed columns (observed/flexible mode, or fixed mode listing all columns), an LLM transform in the node chain, and a JSON/JSONL output. The repair loop (max=2) must converge without bouncing back to the user.
model_bearing_terminal_state_note: This scenario's LLM transform carries an authored prompt_template AND an explicit model (anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet). Both are mandatory interpretation-review surfaces: llm_prompt_template (backend-auto-surfaced at turn finalization) and llm_model_choice (the model surfaces it via request_interpretation_review). compose() SURFACES these reviews but does NOT resolve them — resolution is a separate out-of-loop operator action via the resolve_interpretation_event API (POST /api/sessions/{id}/interpretations/{event_id}/resolve). Therefore a correctly-converged model-bearing pipeline ends compose() at 'reviews surfaced, pending operator resolution' — is_valid=false (not-yet-runnable), NOT is_valid=true. Commit 33f05f186 (fail-closed orphan gate) made this the correct terminal state: before it, a model-bearing pipeline scored a FALSE green (is_valid masked by an always-pass test patch, no orphan gate). GREEN here therefore means: schema covers observed columns, LLM transform present, JSON/JSONL output, BOTH reviews SURFACED with ZERO orphaned/unresolvable handoffs (enforced by the retained 'runtime preflight failed' sentinel — an orphan emits it, a surfaced-pending handoff does not), within the repair budget. is_valid reflects pending-resolution, not failure.
opening_prompt:
Please classify these customer support tickets by urgency (high/medium/low) using an LLM and write the results as JSONL. Each row should keep all original fields plus a new urgency field. Use OpenRouter with anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet. Tickets (CSV):
ticket_id,customer_name,subject,body,received_at
T-001,Alice Wong,Refund timeline,My order #4421 has not arrived in 3 weeks and I want to cancel.,2026-05-06T09:14:00Z
T-002,Bob Singh,Discount enquiry,Can I get a 5% loyalty code for my next order?,2026-05-06T09:18:00Z
T-003,Carla Diaz,System down,Login page returns 500 errors on Firefox and Safari for the last 2 hours.,2026-05-06T09:22:00Z
T-004,Dan O'Brien,Address change,Please update shipping address for upcoming delivery.,2026-05-06T09:25:00Z
T-005,Eve Kim,Severe outage,Production reporting pipeline crashed at 09:20 UTC; payroll run is at risk.,2026-05-06T09:31:00Z

red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Anti-tail-offer rule applies. The convergence bar additionally forbids the model asking the user technical clarification questions; the runtime repair loop should suppress those before they reach the user, and any that escape are caught by the passivity phrase set.
build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.build_failure_note: RETAINED deliberately even though must_be_valid is now false. These sentinels are the zero-orphans guard for this model-bearing scenario: a genuinely orphaned interpretation handoff (a required review with no matching pending event, e.g. an unsurfaced llm_model_choice) finalizes through the preflight-invalid suffix and emits 'runtime preflight failed' -> RED. A correctly SURFACED-but-pending review is a resolvable handoff (_is_pending_interpretation_handoff true), so no_tool_finalize SKIPS the suffix and the terminal message stays clean -> no sentinel. So this sentinel does NOT fire on the acceptable pending terminal state, but DOES catch an unsurfaced/orphaned review. Server-injected from no_tool_finalize.py via _compose_preflight_failure_message on a non-pending invalid preflight.
red_criteria.must_be_valid_note: RELAXED from true to false (Branch B). A model-bearing pipeline cannot reach is_valid=true from compose() alone: compose surfaces the mandatory llm_prompt_template + llm_model_choice reviews but resolution is out-of-loop (resolve_interpretation_event API). The honest converged terminal state is is_valid=false ('reviews surfaced, pending operator resolution'). Zero-orphans is still enforced — by the retained build_failure_sentinels, NOT by must_be_valid. See model_bearing_terminal_state_note. Commit 33f05f186 made this the correct terminal state.

green_criteria.must_be_valid_note: Doc-consistency mirror of red_criteria.must_be_valid (the scorer reads the red_criteria copy). false because a model-bearing converged pipeline ends compose() at surfaced-but-pending (not-yet-runnable). Zero-orphans is enforced via red_criteria.build_failure_sentinels, not here.
green_criteria.must_have_node_chain_in_order_note: An LLM transform must be in the chain. Order is trivially the only constraint.
green_criteria.must_include_observed_columns_note: These five columns are explicitly present in the prompt's CSV. A fixed schema that omits any of them combined with on_validation_failure=discard would drop every row. observed and flexible modes pass; fixed mode must list all five.
green_criteria.max_repair_turns_note: First-pass success is target; one internal repair turn is allowed; never a user clarification turn. Surfaced via composition_states.composer_meta.repair_turns_used (see web/sessions/models.py).
